insight: For Sweden, hydration breaks in the 2026 World Cup have had a mixed impact on their momentum. On average, they managed to gain momentum in half of the breaks, with a slight increase in performance, as indicated by a mean change of +0.100 per minute. However, when they were pressing before a break, their performance actually dipped, with an average change of -0.100 per minute, and they failed to maintain momentum after the break. In contrast, when they weren't pressing before a break, their performance improved significantly, with an average change of +0.300 per minute, suggesting that Sweden may benefit from adopting a more relaxed approach leading into hydration breaks.
